We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Representative

Job Location: Challenge, along Ibadan - Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements


	Minimum of an OND
	0-1 year of experience.
	Must be an intelligent, smart, and outspoken lady who can communicate fluently in English.
	Must be able to handle customer complaints via mobile phones, systems, or social media handles.
	Must be able to handle sales transactions, process payments, and maintain an orderly and clean store floor.
	Proximity to the job location.


Salary
N50,000 - N70,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: leamconsultingnigeria@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: For more information, contact via WhatsApp: 09047710414.",

Customer Representative Job at LEAM Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        LEAM Consulting Limited is the foremost consulting firm that stands out in areas of professional certification training, HR advisory services, Business Development / Startup, and IT consulting services in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Representative

Job Location: Challenge, along Ibadan - Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements
     

            
    




	Minimum of an OND
	0-1 year of experience.
	Must be an intelligent, smart, and outspoken lady who can communicate fluently in English.
	Must be able to handle customer complaints via mobile phones, systems, or social media handles.
	Must be able to handle sales transactions, process payments, and maintain an orderly and clean store floor.
	Proximity to the job location.


Salary
N50,000 - N70,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: For more information, contact via WhatsApp: 09047710414.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Manager",
  "description": "Mshel Homes, was founded in 2018 by a visionary entrepreneur, Mshel Homes began with a small team dedicated to transforming the real estate landscape. Establishing itself as a trusted name, Mshel Homes navigated the challenges of the COVID-19 pandemic by diversifying its offerings to become the most exceptional real estate company.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Manager

Job Location: Salem - Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking an ambitious, results-driven, and highly experienced Sales Manager to lead our sales team and accelerate business growth!
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for developing and executing strategic sales plans, managing and coaching a team of Sales Executives, cultivating key client relationships, and consistently driving revenue targets across all channels.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead, manage, and motivate the sales team to meet and exceed monthly, quarterly, and annual revenue targets.
	Develop and implement comprehensive sales strategies aligned with overall business objectives and market opportunities.
	Monitor individual and team performance through KPIs and sales metrics, providing regular coaching, feedback, and performance reviews.
	Identify and pursue new business opportunities through market research, networking, partnerships, and strategic prospecting.
	Build and maintain strong, long-term relationships with key clients, stakeholders, and strategic accounts.
	Oversee the preparation and presentation of proposals, quotations, and contracts, ensuring alignment with company pricing and policies.
	Lead high-level negotiations and support the team in closing complex or high-value deals.
	Collaborate with the marketing team to align sales campaigns, lead generation activities, and brand messaging.
	Analyse sales data, pipeline reports, and market trends to forecast revenue and identify areas for growth.
	Recruit, onboard, and train new Sales Executives, fostering a high-performance culture within the department.
	Report sales performance, market intelligence, and strategic recommendations to senior management on a regular basis.
	Stay abreast of industry developments, competitor activities, and customer preferences to keep the team competitive.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field; an MBA or postgraduate qualification is an added advantage.
	5&ndash;7 years of progressive sales experience, with at least 2&ndash;3 years in a sales leadership or managerial role.
	Demonstrated track record of meeting and exceeding sales targets and growing revenue.
	Exceptional leadership, coaching, and team management skills with the ability to inspire and develop talent.
	Strong communication, negotiation, and presentation skills at both client and executive levels.
	Strategic thinker with excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and CRM software (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot, or equivalent).
	Self-driven, goal-oriented, and able to thrive in a fast-paced, target-driven environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: hr@mshelhomeslagos.com using the position as the subject of the email.

Note: Proximity to the location will be considered",

Sales Manager Job at Mshel Homes Limited




        
    

        
        Mshel Homes, was founded in 2018 by a visionary entrepreneur, Mshel Homes began with a small team dedicated to transforming the real estate landscape. Establishing itself as a trusted name, Mshel Homes navigated the challenges of the COVID-19 pandemic by diversifying its offerings to become the most exceptional real estate company.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Manager

Job Location: Salem - Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking an ambitious, results-driven, and highly experienced Sales Manager to lead our sales team and accelerate business growth!
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for developing and executing strategic sales plans, managing and coaching a team of Sales Executives, cultivating key client relationships, and consistently driving revenue targets across all channels.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead, manage, and motivate the sales team to meet and exceed monthly, quarterly, and annual revenue targets.
	Develop and implement comprehensive sales strategies aligned with overall business objectives and market opportunities.
	Monitor individual and team performance through KPIs and sales metrics, providing regular coaching, feedback, and performance reviews.
	Identify and pursue new business opportunities through market research, networking, partnerships, and strategic prospecting.
	Build and maintain strong, long-term relationships with key clients, stakeholders, and strategic accounts.
	Oversee the preparation and presentation of proposals, quotations, and contracts, ensuring alignment with company pricing and policies.
	Lead high-level negotiations and support the team in closing complex or high-value deals.
	Collaborate with the marketing team to align sales campaigns, lead generation activities, and brand messaging.
	Analyse sales data, pipeline reports, and market trends to forecast revenue and identify areas for growth.
	Recruit, onboard, and train new Sales Executives, fostering a high-performance culture within the department.
	Report sales performance, market intelligence, and strategic recommendations to senior management on a regular basis.
	Stay abreast of industry developments, competitor activities, and customer preferences to keep the team competitive.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field; an MBA or postgraduate qualification is an added advantage.
	5–7 years of progressive sales experience, with at least 2–3 years in a sales leadership or managerial role.
	Demonstrated track record of meeting and exceeding sales targets and growing revenue.
	Exceptional leadership, coaching, and team management skills with the ability to inspire and develop talent.
	Strong communication, negotiation, and presentation skills at both client and executive levels.
	Strategic thinker with excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and CRM software (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot, or equivalent).
	Self-driven, goal-oriented, and able to thrive in a fast-paced, target-driven environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the position as the subject of the email.

Note: Proximity to the location will be considered    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Vacancies at Staff-Recruit
content:
Staff-Recruit is a leading Human Resource and Management Consulting firm that provides a range of recruitment, selection, outsourcing, training & development solutions. We are a dedicated team of professional consultants offering top-of-the-line executive recruitment and selection services. We cater to the needs of a range of professionals seeking employment and work together to create effective solutions using our networks and strong client base.

STAFF-RECRUIT was founded in 2020 in Lagos, Nigeria. We have served clients across the public and private sectors and various industries, including Banking, Oil and Gas, Insurance, Information Technology, Real Estate, Energy, FMCG, Manufacturing, and many more.
     

            
    



We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Business Development Executive

Job Location: Ajah, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for a resilient, empathic Business development executive who can fit into their job roles without much supervision thereby contributing to company’s growth.
	The business development executives will be responsible for finding and retaining clients, encouraging extant clints to purchase added products or features and remaining abreast of changes in consumption.


Key Responsibilities


	Attracting new clients by innovating and overseeing the sales process for the business.
	Working with senior team members to identify and manage company risks that might prevent growth.
	Identifying and researching opportunities that come up in new and existing markets.
	Preparing and delivering pitches and presentations to potential clients
	Combining efforts and fostering collaborative environment within the business as a whole.
	Familiarizing yourself with all products and services offered by the company.
	Researching the needs of organization and individual online to identify new leads and potential new market.
	Negotiate and renegotiating by phone or in person.
	Attending conferences, meeting and industry events.
	Developing quotes and proposal.
	Contacting potential client via email or phone call to establish rapport and set up meeting.
	Developing sales goal/targets and ensuring they are met.


Requirements


	Degree in Marketing, Business Administration or similar.
	3-5 year of extensive sales experience.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

2.) Admin & Facility Officer

Job Location: Ajah, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Admin. Executive is responsible for overseeing the day-to-day administrative operations of the organization and ensuring the effective management, maintenance, safety, and functionality of all company facilities.
	The role ensures a conducive work environment, efficient use of resources, compliance with policies, and the smooth operation of administrative and facility-related activities.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee daily administrative and facility operations.
	Manage office supplies, equipment, assets, and inventory.
	Supervise support staff, including cleaners, drivers, security personnel, and office assistants.
	Coordinate maintenance, repairs, and upkeep of company facilities.
	Carry out daily facility checks
	Maintain car documents, car usage register and car maintenance
	Supervise support staff (drivers, cleaners and security)
	Ensure the availability and functionality of utilities and workplace resources.
	Manage vendors, contractors, and service providers.
	Monitor facility safety, security, and compliance with company policies.
	Prepare and control administrative and facility budgets.
	Maintain administrative records, reports, and documentation.
	Support procurement, logistics, and office space management.
	Implement cost-saving initiatives and improve operational efficiency.
	Address facility-related issues promptly to ensure a safe and conducive work environment.


Requirements
Qualifications:


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Business Administration, Estate Management, Facility Management, Engineering, or a related field.
	Professional certifications in Facility Management or Administration will be an added advantage.


Experience:


	3–5 years of experience in Administration, Facility Management, or a related role.
	Experience supervising support staff and managing vendors/contractors.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV using to: [email protected] using “ADMIN EXECUTIVE” as the subject of the mail.

 

 

3.) Procurement Executive

Job Location: Ajah, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Preparing plans for the purchase of equipment, services, and supplies.
	Following and enforcing the company's procurement policies and procedures.
	Reviewing, comparing, analyzing, and approving products and services to be purchased.
	Managing inventories and maintaining accurate purchase and pricing records.
	Maintaining and updating supplier information such as qualifications, delivery times, product ranges, etc.
	Maintaining good supplier relations and negotiating contracts.
	Researching and evaluating prospective suppliers.
	Preparing budgets, cost analyses, and reports.
	Work with team members and procurement manager to complete duties as needed.
	Make professional decisions in a fast - paced environment.
	Estimate and establish cost parameters and budgets for purchases.


Requirements and Qualifications


	Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain Management, or related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of proven experience in procurement, sourcing, or supply chain management.
	Strong negotiation and contract management skills.
	Knowledge of procurement best practices and industry trends.
	Ability to analyze data and make informed decisions.
	Proficiency in procurement software and Microsoft Office suite.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.


Skills Required:


	Procurement.
	Negotiation.
	Supplier management.
	Supply chain optimization.
	Cost - saving strategies.
	Contract management.
	Communication.
	Data analysis.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV using to: [email protected] using "Procurement Executive" as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

	We are looking for a resilient, empathic Business development executive who can fit into their job roles without much supervision thereby contributing to company&rsquo;s growth.
	The business development executives will be responsible for finding and retaining clients, encouraging extant clints to purchase added products or features and remaining abreast of changes in consumption.

	Developing sales goal/targets and ensuring they are met.

	3-5 year of extensive sales experience.

Business Development Executive Job at Staff-Recruit




        
    

        
        Staff-Recruit is a leading Human Resource and Management Consulting firm which provides a range of recruitment, selection, outsourcing, training & development solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive

Job Location: Ajah, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are looking for a resilient, empathic Business development executive who can fit into their job roles without much supervision thereby contributing to company’s growth.
	The business development executives will be responsible for finding and retaining clients, encouraging extant clints to purchase added products or features and remaining abreast of changes in consumption.


Key Responsibilities


	Attracting new clients by innovating and overseeing the sales process for the business.
	Working with senior team members to identify and manage company risks that might prevent growth.
	Identifying and researching opportunities that come up in new and existing markets.
	Preparing and delivering pitches and presentations to potential clients
	Combining efforts and fostering collaborative environment within the business as a whole.
	Familiarizing yourself with all products and services offered by the company.
	Researching the needs of organization and individual online to identify new leads and potential new market.
	Negotiate and renegotiating by phone or in person.
	Attending conferences, meeting and industry events.
	Developing quotes and proposal.
	Contacting potential client via email or phone call to establish rapport and set up meeting.
	Developing sales goal/targets and ensuring they are met.


Requirements


	Degree in Marketing, Business Administration or similar.
	3-5 year of extensive sales experience.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Quality Control (QC) Laboratory Officer",
  "description": "Westbridge Energy and Lubricants Limited is an integrated energy solutions provider that operates in the downstream sector of the oil and gas industry. It is a registered company with the basic purpose of lubricant production and oil trading.

We aim to maintain and develop a secure, diverse, profitable and sustainable lubricants business that provides products that consistently meet or exceed the highest requirements. We are unwavering in our commitment to meeting customer needs and fulfilling our responsibility to protect the environment.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control (QC) Laboratory Officer

Job Location: Mowe, Ogun
Employment Type: Full?Time

Job Summary


	The Quality Control Laboratory Officer is responsible for conducting laboratory analyses and inspections to ensure that raw materials, in-process products, and finished goods meet established quality standards, regulatory requirements, and company specifications.
	The role supports manufacturing operations by identifying quality issues, maintaining accurate laboratory records, and ensuring compliance with quality management systems and good laboratory practices.


Key Responsibilities


	Perform physical, chemical, and microbiological tests on raw materials, packaging materials, in-process samples, and finished products according to approved procedures.
	Conduct routine laboratory analyses using standard testing methods and laboratory equipment.
	Record, Review, and interpret test results accurately and promptly.
	Verify that products comply with internal specifications, customer requirements, and applicable regulatory standards.
	Monitor production processes and provide timely feedback to production teams on quality-related issues.
	Investigate non-conforming materials, out-of-specification (OOS) results, and customer complaints related to product quality.
	Prepare and maintain laboratory documentation, including test records, certificates of analysis, and quality reports.
	Ensure calibration, maintenance, and proper operation of laboratory equipment and instruments.
	Maintain laboratory inventory, including reagents, consumables, and reference standards.
	Adhere to Good Laboratory Practices (GLP), health and safety procedures, and environmental regulations.
	Participate in internal audits, external audits, and continuous improvement initiatives.
	Assist in the development, review, and update of standard operating procedures (SOPs) and test methods.
	Support implementation and maintenance of quality management systems such as ISO standards.


Qualifications and Experience


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Chemistry, Biochemistry, Industrial Chemistry, Microbiology, Chemical Engineering, Food Science and Technology, or a related field, Masters&rsquo; Degree is an added advantage.
	Must have 5 &ndash; 10 years of relevant laboratory and Quality Control experience in a manufacturing company.
	Experience with quality management systems and laboratory documentation.
	Familiarity with applicable industry regulations and standards.


Required Skills and Competencies:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Attention to detail and accuracy.
	Knowledge of laboratory testing techniques and equipment.
	Proficiency in data recording, analysis, and report writing.
	Good understanding of statistical quality control principles.
	Ability to interpret specifications, procedures, and technical documents.
	Strong communication and teamwork skills.
	Good organizational and time-management abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and laboratory information management systems (LIMS).
	Percentage of tests completed within specified turnaround times.
	Number of out-of-specification results investigated and resolved.
	Laboratory compliance with audit requirements.
	Accuracy and completeness of laboratory records.
	Equipment calibration and maintenance compliance rate.
	Reduction in product defects and customer complaints.
	Adherence to safety and regulatory requirements.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to admin@westbridgeindustries.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-07-19",

Quality Control (QC) Laboratory Officer Job at Westbridge Energy and Lubricants Limited




        
    

        
        Westbridge Energy and Lubricants Limited is an integrated energy solutions provider that operates in the downstream sector of the oil and gas industry. It is a registered company with the basic purpose of lubricant production and oil trading.

We aim to maintain and develop a secure, diverse, profitable and sustainable lubricants business that provides products that consistently meet or exceed the highest requirements. We are unwavering in our commitment to meeting customer needs and fulfilling our responsibility to protect the environment.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control (QC) Laboratory Officer
     

            
    



Job Location: Mowe, Ogun
Employment Type: Full?Time

Job Summary


	The Quality Control Laboratory Officer is responsible for conducting laboratory analyses and inspections to ensure that raw materials, in-process products, and finished goods meet established quality standards, regulatory requirements, and company specifications.
	The role supports manufacturing operations by identifying quality issues, maintaining accurate laboratory records, and ensuring compliance with quality management systems and good laboratory practices.


Key Responsibilities


	Perform physical, chemical, and microbiological tests on raw materials, packaging materials, in-process samples, and finished products according to approved procedures.
	Conduct routine laboratory analyses using standard testing methods and laboratory equipment.
	Record, Review, and interpret test results accurately and promptly.
	Verify that products comply with internal specifications, customer requirements, and applicable regulatory standards.
	Monitor production processes and provide timely feedback to production teams on quality-related issues.
	Investigate non-conforming materials, out-of-specification (OOS) results, and customer complaints related to product quality.
	Prepare and maintain laboratory documentation, including test records, certificates of analysis, and quality reports.
	Ensure calibration, maintenance, and proper operation of laboratory equipment and instruments.
	Maintain laboratory inventory, including reagents, consumables, and reference standards.
	Adhere to Good Laboratory Practices (GLP), health and safety procedures, and environmental regulations.
	Participate in internal audits, external audits, and continuous improvement initiatives.
	Assist in the development, review, and update of standard operating procedures (SOPs) and test methods.
	Support implementation and maintenance of quality management systems such as ISO standards.


Qualifications and Experience


	Bachelor's Degree in Chemistry, Biochemistry, Industrial Chemistry, Microbiology, Chemical Engineering, Food Science and Technology, or a related field, Masters’ Degree is an added advantage.
	Must have 5 – 10 years of relevant laboratory and Quality Control experience in a manufacturing company.
	Experience with quality management systems and laboratory documentation.
	Familiarity with applicable industry regulations and standards.


Required Skills and Competencies:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Attention to detail and accuracy.
	Knowledge of laboratory testing techniques and equipment.
	Proficiency in data recording, analysis, and report writing.
	Good understanding of statistical quality control principles.
	Ability to interpret specifications, procedures, and technical documents.
	Strong communication and teamwork skills.
	Good organizational and time-management abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and laboratory information management systems (LIMS).
	Percentage of tests completed within specified turnaround times.
	Number of out-of-specification results investigated and resolved.
	Laboratory compliance with audit requirements.
	Accuracy and completeness of laboratory records.
	Equipment calibration and maintenance compliance rate.
	Reduction in product defects and customer complaints.
	Adherence to safety and regulatory requirements.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
